note: Curated music/audio source candidates for Opal (has a radio tab + youtube/music path). Split by legality: `legal_api` are free/licensed APIs safe to wire directly; `stream_rippers` pull from paid services (Qobuz/Tidal/Deezer) and are the piracy-class ones. `lyrics` is a standout freebie. Source: FMHY audio list. None in the live manifest yet.

legal_api.name: LRCLIB
legal_api.notes: LEGAL, free, keyless lyrics API. Highest-value + lowest-effort music add — pairs with any now-playing track.

legal_api.name: cobalt
legal_api.notes: Open-source media downloader API (YouTube/SoundCloud/etc. → direct audio). A TOOL more than a source, but a clean way to fetch audio for the music path. Self-host to avoid public-instance rate limits.

legal_api.name: Bandcamp
legal_api.notes: Legal, artist-licensed. Good for discovery + streaming free/name-your-price tracks.

legal_api.name: Audius
legal_api.notes: Fully legal decentralized music, open API. Strong fit for a streaming music tab.

legal_api.name: KHInsider
legal_api.notes: Video-game soundtrack archive (MP3/FLAC). Niche but durable and popular.

stream_rippers.name: lucida
stream_rippers.notes: Rips lossless from Qobuz/Tidal/Deezer/Amazon. Piracy-class (paid-service extraction) — gate like a torrent source if wired.

stream_rippers.name: DoubleDouble
stream_rippers.notes: Same class as lucida — paid-service ripper. Piracy-class.

stream_rippers.name: deemix
stream_rippers.notes: Deezer downloader (self-host/library). Paid-service extraction — piracy-class, gate like the others. awesome-piracy.

note_soulseek: The classic p2p music source (Soulseek/slskd) is a protocol, not a URL endpoint — it would need a client integration, not a plugin entry.
